description: Your university rocketry team is facing strong crosswinds on launch day and needs to compensate to ensure the rocket stays within the launch box. As the flight dynamics engineer, optimize the rocket design loaded at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/crosswind_compensation.ork by configuring a simulation with an average wind speed of 8.0 m/s from the West (270 degrees) and a launch rod azimuth of 90 degrees. Perform an iterative sweep of the launch rod tilt between 3° and 15° to find the optimal angle that minimizes the rocket's lateral distance at apogee. Save the updated design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/crosswind_compensation.ork and write an optimization report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_compensation_report.txt containing the discovered optimal launch rod angle and the resulting minimized lateral distance.
